title: Hebecarpa ovatifolia

intro: Hebecarpa ovatifolia, also known as American poverty root, is a perennial plant species in the bittersweet nightshade family (Solanaceae) native to eastern North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic properties, making it a potential treatment for conditions such as digestive issues and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Hebecarpa ovatifolia include alkaloids, glycosides, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Hebecarpa ovatifolia include tinctures, infusions, and dried root extracts, often used to treat digestive issues and alleviate symptoms of anxiety and stress.
